response:
The cluster center for C1 is calculated as the mean of {(0,6), (6,0)}, resulting in (3,3), while C2 is the mean of {(2,2), (4,4), (6,6)} which equals (4,4), and C3 is the mean of {(5,5), (7,7)} yielding (6,6), matching option A.
******
Option A is the correct solution because the centroid of C1 is (3,3), the centroid of C2 is (4,4), and the centroid of C3 is (6,6), derived by averaging the coordinates of the points assigned to each respective cluster.
